title: chrysophyllum delphinense
intro: Chrysophyllum delphinense, also known as the white star apple, is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chrysophyllum delphinense include reducing inflammation, fighting against infections, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Chrysophyllum delphinense can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
